refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement boundary (what constitutes 'available'), (2) the evaluation period (e.g., 30 days, 90 days, 1 year), (3) exclusions (planned maintenance windows, customer-initiated pauses), and (4) the role of safety-driven shutdowns. High safety criticality demands that availability not be achieved at the expense of safety; therefore, safety-driven unavailability is acceptable and must be excluded from the penalty calculation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational telemetry (uptime/downtime events, root causes, duration) from deployed NGHE units over the agreed evaluation period. Calculate availability as (Total Scheduled Time − Unplanned Downtime − Safety-Driven Shutdowns) / Total Scheduled Time. Compare against the ≥0.97 threshold. Conduct root-cause analysis on any downtime events to identify systemic failure modes.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults, anomalies) with timestamp, duration, and root-cause classification.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall implement predictive and preventive maintenance strategies to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time availability status and historical availability metrics to the Site Manager and Project Manager.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure that safety-driven shutdowns are logged separately and excluded from availability penalty calculations.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and analytics engine that collects telemetry from all Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, detects downtime events in real-time, logs root causes, and computes availability KPI against a configurable target threshold.

DP-B: Distribute availability monitoring across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules independently, with each module reporting its own uptime/downtime to a lightweight aggregator that sums availability metrics.

DP-C: Implement a redundant architecture (dual Vehicle units, failover Teleoperation links) such that the NGHE solution as a whole maintains availability even if individual components fail, with availability measured at the solution level only.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and analytics engine that collects telemetry from all Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, detects downtime events in real-time, logs root causes, and computes availability KPI against a configurable target threshold.

potential_sources: DP-A depends on reliable Telemetry interface from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; if Telemetry is lossy or delayed, availability calculation may be inaccurate.
DP-A requires SafetyAlert interface to distinguish safety-driven shutdowns from unplanned failures; coupling to Safety Services is necessary.
DP-A couples to Tasking interface to understand scheduled vs. unscheduled downtime windows.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is acceptable because these dependencies are well-defined, traceable interfaces. DP-A is decoupled from the internal design of Vehicle and Teleoperation; it only consumes their telemetry outputs. This is a clean, information-flow coupling rather than a structural or control coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: All Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems emit Telemetry at sufficient frequency and fidelity to detect downtime events within acceptable latency (e.g., <5 seconds).
Assumption: SafetyAlert interface reliably signals safety-driven shutdowns so they can be excluded from availability penalty.
Risk: If Telemetry is incomplete or delayed, availability calculation will be inaccurate, leading to false positives/negatives in KPI reporting.
Risk: If root-cause classification is manual or subjective, availability analysis may be inconsistent across evaluation periods.
Risk: High safety criticality means that any false negative (failing to detect a safety-driven shutdown) could mask a safety issue.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Strengthen the DP by explicitly requiring: (1) automated root-cause classification rules (e.g., 'Vehicle telemetry loss for >10 seconds = Vehicle unavailable'), (2) SafetyAlert integration with clear semantics for safety-driven vs. unplanned downtime, (3) configurable evaluation period and threshold to support customer-specific targets, and (4) audit trail for all availability calculations to support verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the availability monitoring and analytics engine, receives telemetry and safety alerts from subordinate modules, and exposes availability KPI metrics to external stakeholders (Site Manager, Project Manager). The module is responsible for end-to-end availability measurement, root-cause analysis, and KPI reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational telemetry (uptime/downtime events, timestamps, subsystem status) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Used to detect and log unplanned downtime events.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-driven shutdown signals from Safety Services. Used to distinguish safety-driven unavailability from unplanned failures and exclude it from availability penalty.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives scheduled task/maintenance windows from Site Manager or Project Manager. Used to define the evaluation period and exclude planned downtime from availability calculation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of availability KPI because it integrates all subsystems and has visibility into the entire system state. The availability monitoring engine logically belongs at the solution level, not distributed across Vehicle or Teleoperation. Minimal diffusion: only the analytics engine and KPI reporting logic are contained; telemetry collection is delegated to subordinate modules.

Vehicle could own availability monitoring for its own subsystems (e.g., propulsion, steering), but it cannot measure availability of the entire NGHE solution (which includes Teleoperation and Safety Services). Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would require Vehicle to have knowledge of Teleoperation and Safety Services state, creating high diffusion and coupling. Not recommended.

Teleoperation could own availability monitoring for its own communication links and control logic, but it cannot measure availability of Vehicle or Safety Services. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would create similar diffusion and coupling issues as Vehicle. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety-driven shutdown logic and SafetyAlert generation. It could contribute to availability monitoring by providing safety-driven shutdown signals, but it should not own the overall availability KPI calculation because that requires integration across all subsystems. Safety Services should be a data provider, not the owner of DP-A.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the optimal owner of DP-A (centralized availability monitoring and analytics engine) because: (1) it has the broadest system scope and visibility into all subsystems, (2) availability is a system-level KPI that cannot be decomposed to individual subsystems, (3) it naturally integrates telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation and safety alerts from Safety Services, and (4) it minimizes diffusion by centralizing the analytics logic in one place rather than distributing it across multiple modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module becomes a 'god object' that owns too many responsibilities, it may become difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Clearly separate the availability monitoring engine from other NGHE Solution responsibilities (e.g., system orchestration, user interface). Consider a dedicated Availability Analytics sub-module within NGHE Solution.

Risk: If Telemetry or SafetyAlert interfaces are unreliable or incomplete, the availability calculation will be inaccurate. Mitigation: Define strict interface contracts (e.g., Telemetry must be emitted at least once per 5 seconds, SafetyAlert must be emitted before any safety-driven shutdown). Implement health checks to detect interface failures.

Risk: If the evaluation period or threshold is not configurable, the NGHE Solution cannot adapt to customer-specific availability targets. Mitigation: Implement configuration management to allow Site Manager or Project Manager to set evaluation period and threshold via Tasking interface.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the IM assignment by: (1) explicitly defining a sub-module within NGHE Solution called 'Availability Analytics Engine' that owns the monitoring and KPI calculation logic, (2) specifying the interface contracts for Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Tasking (e.g., frequency, latency, data format), (3) defining the configuration parameters (evaluation period, threshold, root-cause classification rules) that must be exposed to Site Manager and Project Manager, and (4) establishing a clear ownership boundary between Availability Analytics Engine and other NGHE Solution components (e.g., system orchestration, user interface).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized availability monitoring and analytics engine) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP specifies a concrete solution approach: collect telemetry, detect downtime events, log root causes, and compute KPI. The DP is measurable and testable. No decoupling or independence issues detected; the DP is tightly coupled to the FR as expected for a KPI requirement.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If Telemetry interface is not standardized across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, the Availability Analytics Engine will need to implement multiple telemetry parsers, increasing complexity and diffusion. Mitigation: Enforce a single Telemetry interface contract across all subsystems.

Diffusion Risk 2: If SafetyAlert interface is not clearly defined (e.g., what constitutes a 'safety-driven shutdown' vs. an 'unplanned failure'), the Availability Analytics Engine will need to implement heuristics or manual classification, increasing diffusion. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert semantics explicitly in the interface contract.

Diffusion Risk 3: If Tasking interface does not provide clear information about scheduled maintenance windows, the Availability Analytics Engine will need to infer them from telemetry, increasing complexity. Mitigation: Require Tasking to explicitly signal maintenance windows.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the clear owner of DP-A. The module has the broadest scope and visibility into all subsystems. Containment score of 95 indicates minimal diffusion. The three external interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) are well-defined and traceable. No evidence of diffusion across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules. Ownership is unambiguous.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible through operational telemetry collection and KPI calculation. The Availability Analytics Engine (owned by NGHE Solution) will compute availability metrics that can be compared against the ≥0.97 threshold. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of all subsystems to emit Telemetry, (2) integration of SafetyAlert from Safety Services, (3) configuration of evaluation period and threshold via Tasking, and (4) audit trail for all availability calculations. All of these are within the scope of NGHE Solution ownership.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the availability monitoring and analytics engine. Vehicle and Teleoperation are responsible for emitting Telemetry. Safety Services is responsible for emitting SafetyAlert. Site Manager and Project Manager are responsible for configuring evaluation period and threshold via Tasking. No ambiguity or overlap detected.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Strengthen the Telemetry interface contract to specify frequency, latency, and data format requirements. Ensure all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation) emit Telemetry at the same cadence and format.

Revision 2: Strengthen the SafetyAlert interface contract to define the semantics of safety-driven shutdowns. Provide clear examples of what constitutes a safety-driven shutdown vs. an unplanned failure.
Revision 3: Strengthen the Tasking interface contract to require explicit signaling of scheduled maintenance windows. Define the format and timing of maintenance window notifications.
Revision 4: Define a sub-module within NGHE Solution called 'Availability Analytics Engine' to clarify ownership and reduce the risk of NGHE Solution becoming a 'god object'.

Revision 5: Establish acceptance criteria for availability calculation accuracy (e.g., 'availability calculation shall be accurate to within ±1% of true availability'). Define how accuracy will be verified.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.4) that address specific aspects of availability: downtime detection, preventive maintenance, KPI reporting, and safety-driven shutdown handling. Each sub-FR is more atomic and can be independently designed and verified. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify the design and reduce risk.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults, anomalies) with timestamp, duration, and root-cause classification.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall implement predictive and preventive maintenance strategies to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time availability status and historical availability metrics to the Site Manager and Project Manager.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure that safety-driven shutdowns are logged separately and excluded from availability penalty calculations.

assumptions: Assumption 1: All Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems emit Telemetry at sufficient frequency (at least once per 5 seconds) and fidelity to detect downtime events within acceptable latency.
Assumption 2: SafetyAlert interface reliably signals safety-driven shutdowns so they can be distinguished from unplanned failures.
Assumption 3: Tasking interface provides clear information about scheduled maintenance windows and evaluation periods.
Assumption 4: Root-cause classification can be automated using predefined rules (e.g., 'Vehicle telemetry loss for >10 seconds = Vehicle unavailable').
Assumption 5: The customer-defined availability target (≥0.97) is achievable with the current NGHE architecture and does not require redundancy or failover mechanisms.
Assumption 6: Safety-driven shutdowns are acceptable and will not be penalized in availability calculations.
open_risks: Risk 1: If Telemetry interface is lossy or delayed, availability calculation may be inaccurate, leading to false positives/negatives in KPI reporting.
Risk 2: If SafetyAlert interface is not clearly defined, the Availability Analytics Engine will need to implement heuristics or manual classification, increasing complexity and diffusion.
Risk 3: If root-cause classification is manual or subjective, availability analysis may be inconsistent across evaluation periods.
Risk 4: High safety criticality means that any false negative (failing to detect a safety-driven shutdown) could mask a safety issue.
Risk 5: If NGHE Solution module becomes a 'god object' that owns too many responsibilities, it may become difficult to test and maintain.
Risk 6: If the evaluation period or threshold is not configurable, the NGHE Solution cannot adapt to customer-specific availability targets.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Telemetry interface specification with frequency, latency, and data format requirements.
Evidence 2: SafetyAlert interface specification with clear semantics for safety-driven shutdowns.
Evidence 3: Tasking interface specification with support for scheduled maintenance windows and evaluation period configuration.
Evidence 4: Root-cause classification rules and automated detection logic.
Evidence 5: Availability calculation algorithm and KPI reporting format.
Evidence 6: Acceptance criteria for availability calculation accuracy.
Evidence 7: Test plan for availability monitoring and KPI calculation.
Evidence 8: Operational telemetry data from deployed NGHE units to validate availability calculation.
